page: Microsoft Azure B16ls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bsv2 server family. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture. The server features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2.0 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz and includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate average tier capabilities across standard operations. The instance achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1898.09 and a multi-core score of 15532.58. In Geekbench testing, it recorded a single-core score of 1652.0 and a multi-core score of 9950.0, with software compilation scoring 15309.0 and ray tracing reaching 17585.0. Memory bandwidth testing showed read-only small block cached speeds of 833.78 GB/sec, while large block uncached read and write speeds were 99.55 GB/sec and 56.79 GB/sec, respectively. For database and key-value workloads, the server processed 15.93 million Redis SET operations per second and achieved a PassMark database operations score of 7904.75. Static web serving throughput reached 21.38 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the burstable CPU allocation model makes this instance suitable for workloads with variable CPU utilization, offering a lower cost profile compared to continuously provisioned resources. However, the burstable nature introduces performance tradeoffs if the CPU credit balance is exhausted during sustained high-load events. Additionally, the low memory-to-vCPU ratio limits its suitability for memory-intensive applications. This server is best suited for workloads such as lightweight web serving, software compilation, development and testing environments, and small-scale database operations that experience intermittent traffic spikes.
description: Microsoft Azure B16ls_v2 is an x86_64 burstable virtual machine from the Bsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, featuring 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of RAM. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results show a PassMark CPU score of 21273.81, a Geekbench multi-core score of 9950.0, and a static web serving throughput of 21.38 GB/sec. It also handles Redis SET operations at 15.93 million operations per second. Due to its burstable CPU allocation and 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server offers a cost-effective solution for workloads with fluctuating resource demands. It is best suited for development environments, code compilation, lightweight web serving, and database operations with variable traffic patterns.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B16ls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, optimized for workloads with variable CPU utilization.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B16ls_v2 is a burstable x86_64 VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, designed for variable-demand workloads.
tagline: A burstable, Intel-powered virtual machine with sixteen vCPUs and thirty-two gigabytes of memory for handling variable and intermittent workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores
Equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
Burstable CPU allocation model designed for variable-demand workloads
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage
Delivers 15.93 million Redis SET operations per second
Best suited for development environments, code compilation, and web serving
